meta.setting: contested mining claim line on a brushy Jefferson hillside
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in claim-jump tension and rough legal evidence

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Mining claims and settlement pressure around Whiskeytown.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Gold-rush material context and legal friction.

image_prompt.subject: split cedar claim stakes with tacked paper notices, a survey chain, cut brush, boot tracks, and two armed men arguing at the edge of frame
image_prompt.scene: windy hillside after rain; one stake leans, the paper is wet and unreadable, mule ears show beyond brush, no legible text
image_prompt.composition: square close landscape plate with the claim stakes large in foreground and disputing figures smaller beyond them
image_prompt.camera: low ground-level view from beside the stakes, making the boundary feel immediate and dangerous
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make a few sticks and scraps of paper carry the weight of a whole gunfight.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine evidence scene
render_traits: slightly simplified men, mule, brush, stakes, and trail objects
modest texture resolution on wet paper, cedar, mud, cloth, metal, and brush
hard ambient occlusion under stakes, chain, boots, brush, and mule legs
cool post-rain light with exposed hillside wind
foreground evidence composition with background confrontation
style_modifiers.palette: rain-dark cedar, gray paper, iron black, buckskin tan, manzanita red-brown, wet soil umber, and pale overcast sky.

text_rendering.instructions: Claim notices may exist but must be blurred, torn, or unreadable; no legible writing.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The claim stakes, wet notice, chain, boot tracks, brush, and confrontation are readable.
Every object belongs plausibly to an 1899 mining-claim dispute.
The image makes the boundary feel legally small and socially explosive.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
